segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens on a city street where two men walk, one in a teal tie-dye hoodie and the other in a black hoodie, both holding bananas and engaging in playful banter. The man in the teal hoodie exclaims 'Jam!' followed by the man in the black hoodie asking, 'Why are you trying to eat my bread, fam?' and the teal-hoodied man retorting, 'Why do you do it in videos?' The scene then shifts to a close-up of a hand squeezing ketchup onto a plate, followed by a different man, also in a black hoodie, eating a slice of pizza at an outdoor café, commenting, 'I feel so fucking sick' and later, 'This is grim,' while another voice reacts with 'My God.' The setting transitions to a large, open, covered public space in London, identifiable by 'Nespresso' and 'Hippodrome Casino' signage and a red double-decker bus. Here, a group of five casually dressed men, including the pizza eater, are gathered. They appear to be filming, with one man in a black tracksuit holding a camera on a stabilizer. The man who was eating pizza, now identified as the main speaker, announces, 'Welcome to another Sidemen race,' explaining the challenge: 'eating a different food for every letter in the alphabet.' He details the rules, stating they'll be 'splitting into two teams' with 'three and a half hours or so' to complete the task, emphasizing that they 'can't go to the same place twice' and 'everyone on the team has to eat the entirety of the item.' The group interacts lively, gesturing and laughing, while one man in a light blue hoodie checks his phone. The segment concludes with a surprised 'Oh, fuck!' from one of the group members.

The Man in white hoodie raises his beer glass in a toast, while the Man in black hoodie (first) with 'SOMN' on his chest talks animatedly, and the Man in black hoodie (second) with the green circular logo occasionally films. Their discussion includes a voice suggesting "I think they're finished," met with disagreement, "Finished? No. Even dragging them along," and another remarking, "I don't think they've had a beer," followed by repeated exclamations of "chess." The scene briefly cuts to the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie walking on an urban sidewalk, eating McDonald’s fries and holding a burger, as a voice exclaims "Ham!" and then comments, "Can't get ham in there though." Returning to the restaurant, laughter is heard as a voice points out, "Can we pick up on the fact that how Harry holds a glass?" prompting a challenge, "How would you pick up this?" followed by exclamations of disbelief and laughter, "Look at that! That's how he picks up a glass! What is going on?" The conversation continues with a voice asking, "Boys, I've ever seen you drop a glass like this?" and another instructing, "Use your thumb. Use your thumb," before someone states, "It just doesn't feel natural at all," amidst more laughter. The camera then shifts to an overcast, wet urban sidewalk, showing the Man in black hoodie (first) and the Man in black hoodie (second) standing near a black 'TELEPHONE' booth with a 'WiFi here' sign, a red double-decker bus passing in the background. The Man in black hoodie (first) gestures while speaking, and the Man in black hoodie (second) holds a phone. Their conversation shifts to food, with a voice asking, "Are you searching up, Josh?" and another describing a mix as "apple, banana, chocolate," which is then dismissed as "not good mixes."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on a bustling city sidewalk in front of the Hippodrome Casino, where three men walk and converse amidst the sounds of urban activity. The man in the black 'SOMN' hoodie remarks, "We're on P," and later, "The boys are defeated now. I feel like we're walking in slow motion." The man in the teal tie-dye hoodie, who has a slightly higher-pitched voice, adds, "I want to keep up," and then, "I'm heavy. I'm heavy." The man in the black hoodie with the green circular logo, speaking with a lively tone, humorously observes, "There's about 15 different foods that should not be together and they're inside us right now," eliciting laughter. The scene transitions to the group seated at an outdoor restaurant, enjoying pizza and soda. The man in the black 'SOMN' hoodie, with a deeper voice, questions, "Why do we do eating videos?" and "Why do you guys watch eating videos so much?" The teal hoodie man responds, "I don't know, bro." The green logo man explains, "Every time we're like, at the end of an eating video, we say we're never doing an eating video like that again," to which the 'SOMN' man interjects, "And then it gets 10 million views." The man in the teal hoodie stands, gestures, and laughs, declaring, "If this video don't bang, bro, I'm I'm switching it, you lot. You lot fucking pagans." The green logo man laughs heartily and counters, "No, but if it doesn't bang, we can stop eating for content." The teal hoodie man then states, "I hate eating." The 'SOMN' man, leaning back, shares, "However, like I said, we have intel. And apparently, they're lagging behind," with the green logo man adding, "I wouldn't be surprised." The 'SOMN' man continues, "So we're taking this time now to sit and just digest." The green logo man advises, "Look, it's a marathon, not a sprint," as the 'SOMN' man plans, "We're planning our next..." The teal hoodie man sips from his water bottle, and the 'SOMN' man takes a large bite of pizza, lamenting, "I'm not going to be able to do it off this." The teal hoodie man suggests, "Quinoa," which the green logo man approves, "Quinoa's good." The 'SOMN' man groans, "Another carbohydrate, for fuck's sake!" The green logo man concludes, "We've had a lot of everything, man," while the 'SOMN' man observes, "It looks like decent pizza." Throughout, the lively atmosphere is punctuated by laughter and the sounds of the city and restaurant, with the on-screen text '15/26' visible.

The video opens in an overcast Trafalgar Square, London, with the iconic National Gallery and Nelson’s Column in the background, as a group of five casually dressed men gather around a large fountain. The atmosphere is immediately lively with excited chatter and laughter, as someone repeatedly asks, "is this fun?" The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie is seated on the fountain’s edge, laughing heartily and gesturing animatedly while speaking, his energy contributing to the group's jovial mood. Beside him, the Man in black hoodie (first) and the Man in black hoodie (second), who holds a phone, are also seated, engaged in the continuous, joking conversation. The Man in light blue hoodie and the Man in white hoodie stand nearby, smiling and laughing, occasionally gesturing toward the seated trio. The group discusses past events, with one person recalling, "We got to K," and another asking, "Did you do K or not?" followed by laughter and a declaration, "that's my brain's gone, bro." They recount a story about trying to get "quesadillas" but finding the place "shut," leading to exclamations like "I'm fucked!" and deciding to "sack it off" and sit at the fountain. Celebratory shouts of "Oh, so we won!" and "Congratulations!" erupt, punctuated by more laughter. The conversation shifts to a humorous anecdote, with someone asking, "Why are we laughing at P? Because he goes, 'pineapple!'" prompting further amusement. The group continues to share stories, noting, "We worked out we averaged the food every 14 minutes" and "we were just eating on the go." A whimsical digital overlay of a giant ice cream sundae with a cherry on top floats above the fountain, adding a playful, surreal element to the scene. As a red double-decker bus passes and pigeons scatter across the plaza, the camera dynamically captures the group's expressions and interactions, highlighting their evident camaraderie and lighthearted social gathering in the grand historic setting. They conclude their discussion with a playful debate, "You may think that we won, but I think we actually lost," followed by confident assertions of "I feel great!" and more laughter, underscoring their relaxed and fun-filled interaction.
